Commodity Credit Corporation Fund; Disaster Reserve Assistance Program, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 105–18, title II111 Stat. 170June 12, 1997“That notwithstanding any other provision of law, beginning on , grain in the disaster reserve established in the Agricultural Act of 1970 [see Short Title of 1970 Amendment note set out under ] shall not exceed 20 million bushels”.October 1, 1997section 1281 of this title

children.heading: Establishment, maintenance and disposal by Secretary; amount and nature of reserve
children.label: a Establishment, maintenance and disposal by Secretary; amount and nature of reserve
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Agriculture may under the provisions of this Act establish, maintain, and dispose of a separate reserve of inventories of not to exceed 75 million bushels of wheat, feed grains, and soybeans for the purpose of alleviating distress caused by a natural disaster.Such reserve inventories may include such quantities of grain that the Secretary deems needed to provide for the alleviation of distress as the result of a natural disaster.

children.heading: Acquisition of commodities through price support program
children.label: b Acquisition of commodities through price support program
children.text: The Secretary may acquire such commodities through the price support program. However, if the Secretary determines that no wheat, feed grains, or soybeans are available through the price support program at locations where they may be economically utilized to alleviate distress caused by a natural disaster, the Secretary is authorized to purchase through the facilities of the Commodity Credit Corporation such wheat, feed grains, soybeans, hay, or other livestock forages as the Secretary deems necessary for disposition in accordance with the authority provided in subsection (d) of this section. The Secretary may acquire wheat, feed grains, soybeans, hay, or other livestock forages at such locations, at such times, and in such quantities as the Secretary finds necessary and appropriate and may pay such transportation and other costs as may be required to permit disposition of such wheat, feed grains, soybeans, hay, and other livestock forages under subsection (d) of this section.

children.heading: Prerequisites for sale or disposition of commodities in reserve
children.label: c Prerequisites for sale or disposition of commodities in reserve
children.text: Except when a state of emergency has been proclaimed by the President or by concurrent resolution of Congress declaring that such reserves should be disposed of, the Secretary shall not offer any commodity in the reserve for sale or disposition.

children.heading: Additional authorization for disposition of commodities to relieve distress or for civil defense emergencies
children.label: d Additional authorization for disposition of commodities to relieve distress or for civil defense emergencies
children.text: The Secretary is also authorized to dispose of such commodities only for (1) use in relieving distress (A) in any State, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, Guam, or the Virgin Islands of the United States, (B) in connection with any major disaster or emergency determined by the President to warrant assistance by the Federal Government under the Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act (, as amended; ), and (C) in connection with any emergency determined by the Secretary to warrant assistance under , the Act of (, as amended; note), or section 2267 of this title; or (2) use in connection with a state of civil defense emergency as proclaimed by the President or by concurrent resolution of the Congress in accordance with title VI of The Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act [ et seq.].88 Stat. 14342 U.S.C. 5121section 1427 of this title73 Stat. 5747 U.S.C. 14271242 U.S.C. 5195September 21, 1959See References in Text note below.1So in original. Probably should not be capitalized.2

children.heading: Sale at equivalent prices for maintenance of reserve
children.label: e Sale at equivalent prices for maintenance of reserve
children.text: The Secretary may sell at an equivalent price, allowing for the customary location and grade price differentials, substantially equivalent quantities in different locations or warehouses to the extent needed to properly handle, rotate, distribute, and locate such reserve.

children.heading: Utilization of Commodity Credit Corporation and usual and customary channels, etc., of trade and commerce
children.label: f Utilization of Commodity Credit Corporation and usual and customary channels, etc., of trade and commerce
children.text: The Secretary may use the Commodity Credit Corporation to the extent feasible to fulfill the purposes of this section; and to the maximum extent practicable consistent with the fulfillment of the purposes of this section and the effective and efficient administration of this section shall utilize the usual and customary channels, facilities, and arrangements of trade and commerce.

children.heading: Rules and regulations
children.label: g Rules and regulations
children.text: The Secretary may issue such rules and regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is hereby authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.
